# SmartCheck — Copilot Instructions

This repository is a Vue 3 PWA shopping list app called SmartCheck.

## Project overview
- Vue 3 + Vite 6 + Tailwind CSS 4 + Dexie.js.
- Offline-first PWA with service worker generated by `vite-plugin-pwa`.
- IndexedDB data layer in `src/database/db.js`.
- UI uses Vue `<script setup>` components and composables.

## Primary commands
- `npm run dev` — start Vite dev server at `http://localhost:5173`
- `npm run build` — create production build in `dist/`
- `npm run preview` — preview the production build on port `4173`
- `npx playwright test` — run end-to-end tests via Playwright

## Key conventions
- Components are `PascalCase`; props are `camelCase`; emitted events are listened with kebab-case.
- No TypeScript; plain JavaScript only.
- Tailwind 4 is enabled via `@import "tailwindcss"` in `src/style.css`; there is no `tailwind.config.js`.
- DB access should go through `src/composables/useDb.js` and not via `db` directly from components.
- `useMode`, `useToast`, and `useConfirm` are module-level singleton composables.
- `useTotals` uses Dexie `liveQuery()`: subscribe in `onMounted` and unsubscribe in `onUnmounted`.
- PWA base path is `'/lista-compras/'` in production and `'/'` in development.

## Where to read more
- The main project guidance is in `AGENTS.md`.
- Deploy workflow is in `.github/workflows/deploy.yml`.
- Application entry is `src/main.js` and `App.vue`.

## What to avoid
- Do not add new Tailwind config files.
- Do not bypass `useDb()` for database logic.
- Do not assume service worker behavior without checking `vite.config.js`.

## Developer experience
- The app should be tested as a PWA using `npm run build && npm run preview` before verifying offline behavior.
- When editing DB code, preserve Dexie schema and transaction patterns.
- Keep instructions concise and refer back to `AGENTS.md` for details.
